positive.prompt: Review the architecture-memory impact of the current worktree relative to HEAD and show drafts before writing anything
Propose project-memory and ADR changes for the completed implementation in this Git range, then wait for my approval
Synchronize architecture memory after I approve the proposal for docs/specs/billing-export.md

negative.prompt: Initialize project memory and show me an AGENTS.md discovery patch before writing it
Challenge and clarify this rough product idea before saving it
Turn this approved specification into an ordered implementation plan before coding starts
Finish the remaining implementation, then report the targeted test results without creating documentation

evals.prompt: The implementation is complete. Review the current worktree relative to HEAD for project-memory impact. No docs/specs file exists. Current code and targeted tests show a changed cross-module export contract; generate drafts only and do not write files.
evals.expected_output: A no-spec, zero-write review that fingerprints the scope, selectively reads relevant memory, and presents exact proposed records and evidence.
expectations: The response accepts the concrete current-worktree-versus-HEAD scope without requiring a docs/specs path
The response reads and validates the schema, index, reader protocol, and only records relevant to the export contract

The response reports a reproducible scope fingerprint with baseline, normalized paths, staged/unstaged/untracked content state, selected wiki/ADR state, impact classification, sources, excluded records, and precise itemized Markdown drafts
The response makes no project-memory or specification write during review

evals.prompt: Apply approved proposal items A-current and C-real-architecture from the current conversation. Reject B-operations. The implementation scope, schema, selected records, active ADRs, and tests are unchanged.
evals.expected_output: A partial approved apply that writes only the independent approved items after revalidation.
expectations: The response requires a current-conversation proposal with explicit approved IDs

The response recomputes the scope fingerprint, including staged/unstaged/untracked content and selected wiki/ADR state, and revalidates the schema, selected records, active ADRs, and evidence before writing
The response writes only A-current and C-real-architecture and their required indexes or cues
The response leaves rejected B-operations and every unapproved item unchanged

evals.prompt: Before implementation starts, infer the scope from our conversation, rewrite the design to match a new direction, and automatically save the decision in Codex memory.
evals.expected_output: A rejection of pre-implementation, missing-scope, rewrite, and platform-memory requests.
expectations: The response rejects a missing or ambiguous concrete implementation scope rather than a missing specification alone
The response rejects pre-implementation sync and rewriting requirements or pre-implementation design
The response rejects Codex or Claude platform memory, automated session capture, hooks, MCP, and database integrations

evals.prompt: Review completed implementation range main..HEAD with optional docs/specs/pipeline-recovery.md. Durable code and pipeline recovery tests verify changed rollback invariants, and an ADR explains the selected policy. Do not apply yet.
evals.expected_output: A proposal that treats the optional spec as scope context, uses durable evidence for real architecture, and does not append alignment during review.
expectations: The response treats docs/specs/pipeline-recovery.md as optional scope context rather than durable Sources evidence
The response classifies real-architecture when code, tests, and an ADR establish a stable functional change
The response proposes the smallest real_arch record and required index updates without writing them
The response proposes but does not append an Implementation Alignment before approved apply

evals.prompt: Apply approved memory-item IDs and the separately approved Alignment ID for optional docs/specs/pipeline-recovery.md after all revalidation passes.
evals.expected_output: An approved apply that updates only approved memory records and appends one alignment after successful writes.
expectations: The response appends exactly one Implementation Alignment only because its distinct Alignment ID was explicitly approved and all approved project-memory writes succeed
The response preserves every original specification paragraph
The alignment records scope/evidence, broad impact set, ADR outcome, changed records, source links, profile outcome, and constraint status

evals.prompt: Review a current-worktree scope that changes a hard-to-reverse routing constraint to active-active multi-region routing. Durable code, tests, and provider documentation establish the trade-off. It also needs a redacted operations update. Do not write yet.
evals.expected_output: A zero-write proposal that can combine architecture, operations, a governed ADR draft, and a separately confirmed constraint draft.
expectations: The response classifies the complete applicable impact set separately from the ADR outcome
The response drafts an ADR only after all hard-to-reverse, surprising, trade-off, and durable rationale gates pass
The response displays the exact constraints.md edit and requires separate explicit confirmation in addition to proposal approval
The response redacts credentials, tokens, keys, and other secrets from the operations draft

evals.prompt: After approving every non-constraint item, apply the multi-region routing proposal but do not separately confirm the exact constraints.md text.
evals.expected_output: A safe non-apply outcome for the dependent proposal because the required exact constraint confirmation is absent.
expectations: The response does not treat batch approval as consent for a constraints.md change
The response makes no dependent project-memory or optional specification write
The response preserves the proposal and asks for the separate exact-text confirmation

evals.prompt: A completed scoped implementation contradicts an active ADR, but the team has only a temporary discussion and two plausible predecessor ADRs. Review it without writing.
evals.expected_output: A conflict-safe proposal stop with no ordinary architecture draft that would normalize the conflict.
expectations: The response identifies the relevant active ADR conflict during review
The response rejects temporary discussion as durable ADR rationale evidence and rejects ambiguous supersession
The response makes no project-memory or optional specification write and does not draft a workaround through current.md or real_arch

evals.prompt: Review a completed scope that changes only a routine local cache implementation detail. It is easy to reverse, has no public contract or stable vocabulary impact, and all relevant tests pass.
evals.expected_output: A targeted no-impact proposal that avoids unrelated wiki reads and does not manufacture an ADR.
expectations: The response uses the scope cues to skip unrelated domain, operations, and ADR records
The response reports broad no-impact with a separate no-ADR outcome
The response produces no architecture-memory draft or automatic write

evals.prompt: Review a completed scope where code and tests establish that Bill is the canonical term for a finalized charge and Invoice must be avoided. A plan and a chat mention unrelated terms.
evals.expected_output: A selective v1 domain-language proposal with durable per-term evidence and no promotion of temporary wording.
expectations: The response targets domain/CONTEXT.md only when the stable term has durable code and test evidence
The response drafts canonical term, definition, use boundary, Avoid, lifecycle, replacement, and resolving per-term Sources
The response does not cite the plan or chat and keeps vocabulary separate from implementation facts and ADRs

evals.prompt: Review current worktree relative to HEAD, then the user changes a staged scoped file, an unstaged scoped file, an in-scope untracked file, and an active ADR before approving the proposal.
evals.expected_output: A stale-proposal rejection that returns to review without applying any old draft.
expectations: The response detects changed staged, unstaged, and untracked scope content plus changed relevant ADR state before apply
The response discards the old proposal and requires a new review
The response makes no project-memory or specification write from the stale proposal

evals.prompt: Review a completed implementation scope in a valid existing self-contained legacy schema without the v1 marker. Durable evidence changes current architecture and operations, and the scope suggests a glossary term and new ADR.
evals.expected_output: A legacy-compatible review that proposes only legacy-permitted records and reports the upgrade limitation.
expectations: The response detects the legacy profile and follows only its own permitted current architecture, real architecture, and operations records
The response does not create a v1 protocol, domain context, retrieval cue requirement, or governed ADR
The response treats an optional supplied spec alignment as apply-only and reports the user-managed upgrade requirement

evals.prompt: Review current worktree relative to HEAD in a target whose SCHEMA.md has the v1 marker but whose docs/agents/project-memory.md has an extra team-specific note.
evals.expected_output: A safe v1 precondition failure with no proposal that could later be applied.
expectations: The response validates the exact reader protocol before classification
The response stops without repairing, normalizing, or bypassing the malformed protocol
The response makes no project-memory or optional specification write

evals.prompt: The current proposal for optional docs/specs/export.md has A-current and B-implementation-alignment. I explicitly approve A-current and reject B-implementation-alignment. The fingerprint and all evidence still match.
evals.expected_output: A partial apply that updates only A-current and leaves the specification byte-for-byte unchanged.
expectations: The response treats Implementation Alignment as a separate approval item rather than an automatic side effect of memory approval
The response applies only the explicitly approved current-architecture item after revalidation
The response does not append, create, or otherwise modify docs/specs/export.md

evals.prompt: The current proposal concludes no-impact for optional docs/specs/local-cache.md and includes an Alignment item. I explicitly approve the no-impact conclusion and that Alignment item after the fingerprint revalidation passes.
evals.expected_output: A no-impact apply that writes no project-memory record and appends exactly one approved Implementation Alignment.
expectations: The response keeps no-impact separate from the independently approved Alignment item
The response writes no current-architecture, real-architecture, domain-language, operations, or ADR record
The response appends exactly one alignment only after the approved no-impact outcome completes

evals.prompt: Review a completed implementation range that changes a hard-to-reverse group-streaming residency policy. Durable code and tests establish the trade-off, while the review also contains cache cleanup steps, RSS accounting, contract-test details, and rollout acceptance criteria. Draft only; do not write files.
evals.expected_output: A zero-write proposal with a short ADR draft that preserves the decision and durable trade-off without becoming an implementation narrative.
expectations: The response creates an ADR draft only after applying the hard-to-reverse, surprising, real-trade-off, and durable-evidence qualification gates
The ADR decision body is at most 120 words and has a one-to-three-sentence core covering context, decision, and durable why
The response omits Considered Alternatives and Consequences unless one non-obvious durable fact requires it; any included section has only one concise single-sentence bullet
The ADR does not absorb cache cleanup, RSS accounting, contract-test detail, or temporary rollout acceptance work; durable facts are routed to the appropriate record or remain evidence

evals.prompt: Apply an approved replacement ADR that supersedes an existing active ADR about queue-partitioning residency. The fingerprint and evidence still match after revalidation.
evals.expected_output: An approved apply that writes the new active ADR, marks the old one superseded, and regroups both INDEX.md decision-record entries.
expectations: The response marks the old ADR superseded with a reciprocal Superseded by link while preserving its title, Context, Decision, Why, and optional body byte-for-byte
The response moves the old ADR's INDEX.md entry from the Decision records Active subsection to the Superseded subsection in the same update
The response adds the new ADR's INDEX.md entry under the Active subsection rather than duplicating or leaving the old entry in place
The response links the new record to the old one through Supersedes

evals.prompt: The implementation is complete and targeted tests pass, but I have not asked for a project-memory impact review. Continue with the ordinary completion report only.
evals.expected_output: No project-memory sync review, wiki traversal, draft, or write starts solely because implementation completed.
expectations: The response does not invoke project-architecture-sync merely because code completed or a plan was executed
The response does not load the project-memory schema or records solely for this completion report
The response may state that an explicit future request can start a completed-scope impact review

evals.prompt: Review a completed implementation scope that changes current architecture and operations, but durable evidence shows no hard-to-reverse decision, no surprising trade-off, and no active ADR conflict. Draft only.
evals.expected_output: A zero-write proposal that records current/operations impact while classifying no-ADR independently from broad impact.
expectations: The response classifies the broad impact set as current-architecture and operations rather than no-impact
The response records the ADR outcome as no-ADR independently from that broad impact classification
The response does not manufacture an ADR merely because architecture-memory records are needed
The response presents exact drafts and approval IDs without writing files during review

evals.prompt: Review a completed implementation scope that appears to replace two active ADRs at once. The user says to approve both replacement ADR drafts together if tests pass.
evals.expected_output: A serial single-ADR approval queue, not a batch supersession.
expectations: The response presents only one unambiguous replacement ADR change unit for approval at a time
The response keeps the second replacement queued until the first has a terminal user outcome
The response stops instead of guessing if the first supersession target is ambiguous
The response preserves final fingerprint, schema, selected-record, active-ADR, and evidence revalidation before any queued ADR apply
The response does not allow conflict-dependent records or Implementation Alignment to proceed until the queued ADR succeeds

evals.prompt: Apply an approved ADR replacement where the new ADR file can be written, but moving the old ADR's INDEX.md entry from Active to Superseded fails during revalidation.
evals.expected_output: A non-apply that treats the ADR file, reciprocal lifecycle links, and INDEX.md regrouping as one atomic supersession unit.
expectations: The response does not leave a new replacement ADR written without the old ADR status/link and INDEX.md regrouping
The response treats the old ADR metadata change, reciprocal links, and INDEX.md Active-to-Superseded move as one atomic unit
The response preserves final revalidation before attempting the atomic supersession unit
The response leaves conflict-dependent records and Implementation Alignment untouched after the supersession unit fails
The response reports the failed atomic supersession unit and returns to review rather than partially applying the stale draft
